verification: Did not complete: browser_set_input_files returned ENOENT on the path the subagent constructed; subagent then reported the file as missing.
errors: browser_set_input_files: ENOENT (path passed had ASCII space U+0020 between time and 'PM'; actual filename has Unicode NARROW NO-BREAK SPACE U+202F that macOS Screenshot inserts)

Subagent also reported 'Playwriter extension disconnected' and 'ENOSPC disk space errors' — disk is 99.98% full (~156Mi free of 926Gi) which may have contributed to environment flakiness, but the primary blocker was the U+202F whitespace mismatch.
notes: Subagent tool sequence was structurally correct (status → snapshot → ls → set_input_files → verify) and would have hit the 5-call happy path EXCEPT for the U+202F bug. The path Haiku passed looked identical to the real filename to any human reader, but the on-disk filename uses U+202F where Haiku used U+0020. Full path on disk: 'Screenshot 2026-05-08 at 1.59.53\u202FPM.png'. The orchestrator cannot reliably round-trip this through its own output; the harness needs to resolve the path tolerantly.
haiku_subagent_summary: Located the file and upload element but could not proceed due to system issues (disk space, file accessibility, extension disconnection). The zero-byte file sizes suggest sparse files or filesystem corruption on the test system.
ground_truth_correction: Disk has 156Mi free (99.98% full but functional). File DOES exist at /Users/dosagie/Desktop/Screenshot 2026-05-08 at 1.59.53\u202FPM.png with size 2114352 bytes. Subagent's diagnosis of 'sparse files / filesystem corruption' was a hallucination — the real cause is the U+202F whitespace mismatch.
